titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Oliver Ellsworth Daggett Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This encoded findingaid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 5, 2020 at 23:03

did.abstract: Record books, sermons, writings, and notes document Daggett's ministry and his education and teaching at Yale. Oliver Ellsworth Daggett (1810-1880) served as a Congregational clergyman in Connecticut and New York from 1837 to 1877. He was Chittenden Professor of Divinity and college pastor at Yale from 1867 to 1870.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Oliver Ellsworth Daggett Papers (RG 114), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: Minister at South Church, Hartford, Connecticut
Minister at First Congregational Church, Canandaigua, New York
Chittendon Professor of Divinity and college pastor at Yale
Minister at Second Congregational Church, New London, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This record group provides thorough documentation of the career of a prominent Yale-trained Congregational clergyman of the nineteenth century. Systematic registers of sermons preached and services performed are available, as well as more than 350 full sermons manuscripts and more than 1400 sermon outlines. Notes from Daggett's students years at Yale and sermons from his years as college pastor provide insight into the religious climate at the University.

list.item: II. Sermons, Addresses, and Writings, 1833-1876, n.d.
IV. Personal Items and Memorabilia, 1872, n.d.

did.unittitle: Register of sermon manuscripts: No. 1-361; first delivered 1832-1875. Title and text of sermon listed by Daggett's numbering system with locations and dates preached indicated.

Register of sermon outlines ("extemporaneous or unwritten sermons"): No. 1-1420; register begun in 183, undated towards end. Title and text of sermon outlines listed by a separate numbering sequence; some locations and dates indicated.
Register of Sabbaths and other services: 1833-1880; chronological record of Daggett's preaching activities indicating location and number of sermon manuscript or outline used.
Register of official services in the Church of Christ in Yale College during pastorate of Daggett: 1867 Oct. 31 to 1870 Jul. 18
Numbered according to Daggett's system: No. 1-119
Address at the laying of the cornerstone of the Plymouth Church of Rochester.
Address to pupils of Canandaigua Academy
Address to pupils of Ontario Female Seminary
Address at the funeral of Captain Chas. M. Wheeler
Address on the 200th anniversary of the first church in New London, Connecticut
Articles and exposition of Biblical texts
The Permanent and the Temporary in Scripture
Volume containing "Lectures of Prof. Olmstead on Natural Philosophy" and "Quotations from Prose and Poetry and Remarks of living persons"
Notes Related to Psalms and Hymns
Notes regarding "Percentage of Professors of Religion"
List of OED articles in the Independent
Form of service for extension of "Right Hand of Fellowship"
Copy of letter written by former prisoner to friend still in prison
